steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most effectively emphasizes the size of the crater. Emphasis means the size should feel remarkable — not just stated as a neutral fact, but framed to make the reader perceive it as striking or significant.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Long ago, a crater now known as Hartwell was created when a large object collided with Earth.: mentions the crater but omits its diameter entirely. Nothing about size is emphasized. ✗
  • Choice B — Impact craters, like Hartwell in the United States, are formed when a massive object collides with Earth, creating depressions in the land.: uses “massive” to describe the impacting object, not the crater — and omits the 3.2 km measurement. ✗
  • Choice C — Researchers have investigated the formation of Hartwell, an ancient impact crater measuring 3.2 km in diameter located in the United States.: includes the measurement but presents it as a neutral descriptive detail. No word signals that the size is remarkable. ✗
  • Choice D — When a large object struck what is now the United States less than 500 million years ago, it created Hartwell, a massive crater measuring 3.2 km in diameter.: pairs the evaluative word “massive” directly with the specific measurement “3.2 km in diameter,” making the size feel both concrete and noteworthy. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
